{"query":"The Bear season 5 rotten tomatoes metacritic critic reviews score","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1612,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.rottentomatoes.com/tv/the_bear/s05","title":"The Bear: Season 5 | Rotten Tomatoes","content":"As Carmy fights to transform both the shop and himself, he works alongside a rough-around-the-edges kitchen crew that ultimately reveals itself as his chosen family.
It’s hard to bid farewell to Carmy, Syd, Richie and the rest of the crew who made The Bear a Michelin-star caliber series, but this acclaimed drama goes out with a bang -- having served five show-stopping seasons to savor for years to come.
Peel away the psychological pearl dives through Carmy’s trauma and what this series leaves us with is an avouchment of human possibility, especially when that’s bolstered by people giving their best to ensure your best.
The Bear has made household names of White, Edebiri and Moss-Bachrach — who all give strong performances again in this outing — and fans will no doubt enjoy the ride up to the final episode...
Episode 1 Aired Jun 25, 2026 Soda A storm hits.
Please write a review for your rating to count toward the Popcornmeter.
Once accepted, your review will get its big debut in the Popcornmeter."},{"url":"https://www.metacritic.com/tv/the-bear/season-5/","title":"The Bear season 5 Reviews - Metacritic","content":"Jun 29, 2025 — Based on 25 User Ratings 8.0 88% Positive 22 Ratings 4% Mixed 1 Rating 8% Negative 2. Seven high-tension episodes,
SummaryChef Carmen "Carmy" Berzatto (Jeremy Allen White) returns home to run his family's sandwich shop in Chicago where he faces issues with running a small business, his new employees and his family.
The series transforms a final service into an experience of anxiety and self-discovery for almost every character.
Based on the first seven episodes, "The Bear" still has the opportunity to go down as one of the greatest or worst finales of all time."},{"url":"https://editorial.rottentomatoes.com/article/the-bear-season-5-first-reviews/","title":"The Bear Season 5 First Reviews: An Intense, Entertaining, Satisfying Finale That Secures Its Legacy | Rotten Tomatoes","content":"Critics say the acclaimed series' final season is a back-to-basics return to peak form that takes the show out in style and leaves no room for leftovers.
The fifth and final season of The Bear premieres this week, and the first reviews are now online and are almost unanimously positive.
Christopher Storer’s award-winning series returns to the high-rated yet struggling Chicago restaurant with more chaos and more catastrophe than ever.
For its final season, series creator Christopher Storer goes back to basics.
For me personally, I am bowled over by the performance of Ebon Moss-Bachrach, who plays Cousin Richie.
Liza Colón-Zayas’ Tina… has become the show’s most reassuring presence."},{"url":"https://screenrant.com/the-bear-season-5-rotten-tomatoes-score-debut/","title":"The Bear Season 5 Officially Debuts To Rare Rotten Tomatoes Score","content":"2026 is getting ready to bid farewell to a major Jeremy Allen White series.
As critics are starting to share their reviews for the final season, its Rotten Tomatoes score has debuted at 100% based on 20 reviews.
It's important, however, to remember that The Bear season 5 score on Rotten Tomatoes is subject to change.
As of writing, the audience score has not debuted yet for Christopher Storer's last chapter with these characters.
For now, The Bear season 5's current Rotten Tomatoes score is tied with season 1.
The Bear stars Allen, along with Ayo Edebiri as Sydney, Ebon Moss-Bachrach as Ritchie, Abby Elliott as Natalie, Lionel Boyce as Marcus, Liza Colón-Zayas as Tina, Edwin Lee Gibson as Ebraheim, and Matty Matheson as Neil Fak."}]}

But the reviews are in for Season 5, and the results are far more satisfying. With 25 reviews submitted for the FX series' final season, the Rotten Tomatoes score stands at 100%, with a Metacritic \"must watch\" score of 81 — a return to form for the Emmy winner. For Alison Herman at *Variety*, this season serves as a redemption, with a return to its focus on what made the series great. \"With its storytelling so weighted in favor of moments, tableaus, conversations, and the interiority of some characters more than others, the whole suffers....In its reluctance to drop the pretense that it is a comedy, *The Bear*keeps making the buffoonish Fak brothers (played by Matty Matheson and Ricky Staffieri) sillier and more dissonant with the rest of the show.\".","score":0.945243,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.instagram.com/p/DaA94ApABy9?hl=en","title":"Season 5 is Certified Fresh at 100% on the Tomatometer, with 26 reviews.","content":"The Bear Season 5 has debuted with a near perfect 97% critics' score on Rotten Tomatoes With early reviews calling it an intense and emotional","score":0.8893851,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.imdb.com/news/ni65901216?ref_=nmnw_art_perm","title":"‘The Bear’ Season 5 Rotten Tomatoes Score Is Revealed and It’s Record-Breaking - IMDb","content":"What's on TV & streamingTop 250 TV showsMost popular TV showsBrowse TV shows by genreTV news. ‘The Bear’ Season 5 Rotten Tomatoes Score Is Revealed and It’s Record-Breaking.
